chief_complaint: Exertional chest pain and progressive shortness of breath over 6 weeks
symptoms: Exertional angina (chest tightness with walking >2 blocks, relieved by rest)
Progressive dyspnea on exertion (NYHA Class III)
Unintentional weight gain of 8 lbs in 3 weeks (fluid retention)

diagnosis_codes.description: Atherosclerotic heart disease of native coronary artery without angina pectoris

requested_procedure.name: Transthoracic Echocardiogram (Complete, with Doppler)
requested_procedure.reason: Evaluate cardiac structure and function in setting of new heart failure symptoms with elevated BNP; assess for HFpEF vs HFrEF to guide treatment

prior_treatments: Metformin 1000mg BID — for Type 2 DM, ongoing 4 years
Lisinopril 20mg daily — for hypertension, ongoing 6 years
Atorvastatin 40mg daily — for hyperlipidemia, ongoing 3 years
Furosemide 40mg daily — started 2 weeks ago for edema
clinical_notes: 58-year-old male with multiple cardiovascular risk factors (Type 2 DM, HTN, hyperlipidemia, CKD stage 3) presenting with new-onset heart failure symptoms. BNP markedly elevated at 890 pg/mL confirming hemodynamic stress. Mildly elevated troponin suggests myocardial injury — could be demand ischemia or chronic elevation in the setting of HF and CKD. NYHA Class III symptoms with orthopnea and PND indicate significant functional limitation. Echocardiogram is essential and urgent to: (1) determine ejection fraction to classify HF type (HFrEF vs HFpEF), (2) evaluate for wall motion abnormalities suggesting CAD, (3) assess valvular pathology, (4) guide pharmacotherapy selection. Multiple concurrent comorbidities (DM + CKD + HTN + HF) make this a complex case requiring imaging-guided treatment decisions. Without echo, cannot safely uptitrate guideline-directed medical therapy.
